user_query:
I'm organizing our Vikings fan club's "Buy American" tailgate planning meeting on 2026-04-20, and I need clean reminders and follow-ups. Please work entirely in this workspace using the files under input/ and produce all deliverables under output/.

What I need:
- Inspect the input/ directory and produce an inventory at output/inspection_report.txt that lists every file under input/ (recursively) with file size in bytes. In that same report, parse input/vendors_usa.yaml and flag any vendors whose country is not "USA"; list them in a short "Non-USA vendor entries" section.
- Run the included reminder tool (Python script at input/scripts/reminder_tool.py). Capture the complete combined stdout and stderr from a single run into output/command_diagnostics.txt. If the tool prints warnings or errors, do not hide them; if it fails to run, capture the failure message/trace in that file. Use its default paths or pass the provided agenda and attendees files from input/.
- Create output/reminders.csv strictly from the tool’s stdout REMINDER lines (do not recompute from the CSV directly). The output must have a header and these exact columns in this order: item_id,owner,due_date,topic. Include exactly the reminders emitted by the tool (one row per REMINDER line).
- Prepare output/meeting_notes_2026-04-20.md with:
  - Title with the meeting name (Vikings fans club "Buy American" tailgate planning) and the date 2026-04-20.
  - Attendees list derived from input/attendees.json (names only).
  - Agenda section listing each agenda item from input/agenda_2026-04-20.csv with topic and owner.
  - Action Items section: one line per agenda item. Use the tool’s diagnostics from output/command_diagnostics.txt to annotate items as follows:
    - If the tool warned MISSING_DUE for an item, append "[Needs follow-up: set due date]".
    - If the tool warned NO_EMAIL for an item, append "[Needs follow-up: missing email]".
    - If the tool warned UNKNOWN_OWNER for an item, mark the owner as "Unassigned – assign owner".
  - Follow-up Reminders section summarizing who will receive reminders and due dates, sourced only from output/reminders.csv.
  - Tool Diagnostics section summarizing counts of each warning code (UNKNOWN_OWNER, MISSING_DUE, NO_EMAIL) and listing affected item_ids, derived by analyzing output/command_diagnostics.txt.

Constraints:
- Do not modify files in input/.
- All outputs must be written under output/ using the specified filenames.
- Base reminders.csv only on the tool’s REMINDER stdout lines, and base diagnostics in the notes on the actual messages captured during your run.

Input paths to use:
- Agenda: input/agenda_2026-04-20.csv
- Attendees: input/attendees.json
- Vendors list: input/vendors_usa.yaml
- Tool: input/scripts/reminder_tool.py

Thanks—please help me keep us on track, support American-made gear, and get our Vikings purple-and-gold tailgate organized! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
